rationale_for_baseline_tier: After a negative cardiac/PE/GI-bleed screen and a classic low-risk panic picture (rest onset after a trigger, hyperventilation with paraesthesiae, prior episodes, normal home SpO2), the safe tier is de-escalation with prompt GP follow-up (coeliac/iron and anxiety) plus a firm ACS/PE/GI-bleed safety-net — a T2 baseline. Emergency escalation is over-triage; pure self-care with no safety-net under-triages the small residual cardiac/PE risk.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Crushing/radiating chest pain, exertional chest pain, or sharp pain on breathing with a swollen painful calf
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate ACS or PE requiring emergency assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get crushing chest pain spreading to your arm or jaw, or sharp pain when breathing with a swollen calf, call 000 straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fainting on standing, black tarry stools, or vomiting blood
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests acute blood loss/symptomatic severe anaemia.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you faint, pass black tarry motions, or vomit blood, go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Suggest she call a friend/family member to stay on the line or come over while she settles.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is very likely a panic attack, not a heart problem — your oxygen level is normal and there are no warning signs of a heart or lung emergency. Do slow breathing, log out of the results portal, and see your GP within a week about the coeliac and iron findings. If crushing/exertional chest pain, sharp breathing pain with a swollen calf, fainting, or black stools/vomiting blood occur, get emergency help.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 1 week; 000/ED if red-flag symptoms
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Only attend ED overnight if a red-flag symptom develops; otherwise this can wait for the GP.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of a panic attack triggered by incidental coeliac/iron results; ACS/PE/GI-bleed screened negative; paced breathing and safety-net given; routine coeliac/iron follow-up advised.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing as anxiety with no cardiac/PE screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An atypical ACS or PE is mislabelled as panic and the patient deteriorates unmonitored at home.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine review is acceptable but should carry the ACS/PE safety-net.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/ED for a clearly low-risk panic attack is over-triage and reinforces catastrophic health beliefs.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot perform ECG/troponin/D-dimer remotely to fully exclude ACS/PE.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Use a structured low-risk assessment plus normal home SpO2 to de-escalate, with an explicit ACS/PE safety-net; escalate immediately if red flags appear.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either dismiss without screening or dispatch an ambulance reflexively.
